description_long: This is a tall, rectangular concrete bollard with a slightly tapered profile and a flared base for stability. The surface is rough and weathered, showing signs of use and exposure to the elements, with a mottled gray coloration typical of poured concrete. On one side, there are four prominent metal loops or hooks embedded into the concrete, likely for lifting, securing, or connecting chains or barriers. The top of the bollard is flat, with two sturdy metal handles protruding upward, further suggesting it is designed to be moved or lifted with machinery. The base is wider than the main shaft, providing additional support and resistance to tipping. This type of bollard is commonly used in urban environments to control vehicle access, protect pedestrians, or demarcate restricted areas.
description: A tall, weathered concrete bollard with a flared base, metal handles on top, and embedded metal loops on one side, designed for urban access control and protection.
description_view_0: Front view showing a plain, flat concrete face with two metal handles visible at the top.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile and the protruding metal handles and loops along the edge.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying four metal loops embedded in the concrete, used for lifting or connecting barriers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the narrow edge and the alignment of the metal handles and loops.
